title: cordia globulifera
intro: Cordia globulifera, also known as the "Globe Tree" or "Bilu," is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Cordia globulifera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and aid in the treatment of respiratory infections. Cordia globulifera can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
